title: hieracium pilosella subsp. dasycephaloides
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. dasycephaloides, also known as Mouse-ear Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hieracium pilosella subsp. dasycephaloides include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
